latest.title: Total Voting Rights - Replacement
latest.content:
30 Apr 2026 15:28
RNS Number : 6528C
Digital 9 Infrastructure PLC
30 April 2026
30 April 2026
The following amendment has been made to the 'Total Voting Rights' announcement released on 30 April 2026 at 14:17:13 under RNS No 6385C.
The reference to 'Venture Shares' has been corrected to 'Ordinary Shares'.
All other details remain unchanged. The full amended text is shown below.
DIGITAL 9 INFRASTRUCTURE PLC
("D9" or the "Company")
TOTAL VOTING RIGHTS
The following notification is made in accordance with the UK Financial Conduct Authority Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rule 5.6.1.
On 30 April 2026, the issued share capital of the Company was 541,772,666 Ordinary Shares. The Company does not hold any shares in Treasury. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is 541,772,666.
The above figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
ENDS.
Contacts
Digital 9 Infrastructure plc
Eric Sanderson
via FTI Consulting
InfraRed Capital Partners Limited
James O'Halloran
Mohammed Zaheer
+44 (0) 207 484 1751
Panmure Liberum Limited (Financial Adviser to the Company)
Chris Clarke
Darren Vickers
+44 (0) 203 100 2222
J.P. Morgan Cazenove (Corporate Broker)
William Simmonds
+44 (0) 20 7742 4000
FTI Consulting (Communications Adviser)
Mitch Barltrop
Maxime Lopes
dgi9@fticonsulting.com
+44 (0) 7807 296 032
+44 (0) 7890 896 777
LEI Code: 213800OQLX64UNS38U92
About Digital 9 Infrastructure plc
Digital 9 Infrastructure plc (DGI9) is an investment trust listed on the London Stock Exchange and a constituent of the FTSE All-Share, with the ticker DGI9. The Company's investment objective is to undertake a Managed Wind-Down of the Company and realise all remaining assets in the Company's portfolio in an orderly manner. For more information, please visit
www.d9infrastructure.com
.
About InfraRed Capital Partners (Investment Manager to D9 appointed to effect the Managed Wind-Down)
InfraRed was appointed in an advisory position on 11 October 2024 and AIFM on 11 December 2024 to effect the Managed Wind-Down of D9.
InfraRed manages US$13bn of equity capital for investors around the globe, in listed and private funds across both core and value-add strategies. InfraRed combines a global reach, operating worldwide from offices in London, Madrid, Miami, New York, Sydney and Seoul, with deep sector expertise from a team of more than 160 people. InfraRed is part of SLC Management, the institutional alternatives and traditional asset management business of Sun Life, and benefits from its scale and global platform.
Further details can be found on InfraRed's website
www.ircp.com
.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our
Privacy Policy
.
END

latest.title: Investment Manager Share Purchase
latest.content:
22 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 3858B
Digital 9 Infrastructure PLC
22 April 2026
22 April
2026
DIGITAL 9 INFRASTRUCTURE PLC
("D9" or the "Company")
Investment Manager Share Purchase
Digital 9 Infrastructure plc announces that the Investment Manager, InfraRed Capital Partners Limited ("InfraRed") has purchased 2,893,489 ordinary shares of no par value in the capital of the Company ("Ordinary Shares") at an average price of 4.90 pence per share on 20 April 2026. This purchase of Ordinary Shares was made by InfraRed Partners LLP, a company in the InfraRed Group.
This purchase was made in accordance with the terms of InfraRed's Investment Management Agreement whereby InfraRed is committed to use 10% of its annual management fee (net of applicable taxes) to acquire ordinary shares in the capital of D9 in the secondary market. The Investment Manager, excluding employees or partners, now holds a total of 4,452,358 Ordinary Shares in the Company, representing approximately 0.82% of the total issued share capital.
END

company_30s.what_they_do: DGI9.L is a specialist investment vehicle that holds a basket of shares, primarily in the telecoms and media sector. It manages the assets of a large pension scheme and sells shares to investors through a compulsory redemption scheme.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The fund’s assets are worth billions of pounds, giving it significant influence over the companies it owns. Its performance matters to pensioners, institutional investors and to the market’s perception of the telecoms sector’s valuation.
company_30s.current_state: The fund is trading at a deep discount to its net asset value (NAV). The price is around 6.7p per share while the NAV is about 9.3p, meaning the market is valuing it roughly 30% below what the underlying assets are worth. The fund has been under pressure for months, and its recent announcements focus on restructuring and redemption plans.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show a series of restructuring moves: the director and investment manager have increased their holdings, the fund has announced a compulsory redemption plan, and it secured a new debt facility to support the redemption schedule. These actions signal that the fund is trying to reduce its exposure to under‑performing assets and return value to shareholders.

The announcements cluster around three themes: (1) a push to liquidate and distribute assets, (2) a tightening of capital structure through debt, and (3) a shift in ownership that could bring in new strategic direction.
These moves hint at a stabilization effort; the fund is actively managing its balance sheet and signalling a possible rebound if the market accepts the new structure.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silently hopeful – the chatter is quiet but a few traders see a redemption‑driven rebound.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of bullish posts point to the 28% discount as a buying opportunity, arguing that the fund’s NAV will be realised once the redemption is complete.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish voices focus on the fund’s debt load and the uncertainty around whether the market will trust the new ownership structure, warning that the price could stay stuck below the NAV.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The sentiment has stayed flat; there is no new noise. The lack of chatter suggests the market has not yet priced in any of the restructuring steps, leaving room for surprise.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are dead, confirming that the market is not currently watching this stock. That silence is a green light for a contrarian play if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The compulsory redemption is scheduled to finish in early May. If the fund can complete the sale of its shares and return cash to investors, the price should climb toward the NAV.
Any announcement that the fund will use the debt facility to shore up liquidity could reassure investors and lift sentiment.
A clear statement from the board about a strategic shift or a new partnership could serve as a catalyst for a price rally.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden jump in trading volume around the redemption announcement, a sharp uptick in the share price, and a reduction in the NAV discount. A confirmation would be a 10‑day moving average break above the current price coupled with a volume spike of at least 50% above the 20‑day average.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the late phase of its crash cycle – the price has already fallen 86% and the market has largely written it off. The APEX score (13/21) indicates a tight compression, signalling that the next move could be a breakout if the redemption is completed.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The fund is 86% below its NAV, a classic deep‑crash scenario. In the past, such discounts have recovered to 70–80% of the NAV within a few months when a redemption or restructuring is announced. If the price climbs to the current NAV of 9.3p, that’s a 39% return from today’s 6.7p. If it moves to the midpoint between price and NAV (about 8p), that’s a 20% gain. The mean‑reversion thesis is strong because the market has already priced in the downside; any positive news will trigger a rally.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The fund’s debt load and the uncertainty around whether the market will honour the redemption plan are real risks. If the redemption falls short or the debt facility is not secured, the price could stay stuck below the NAV or even fall further if the fund’s assets are written down. In that worst case, the price could drop to 5p, a 25% loss from today’s level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 39% if the price hits NAV. Potential downside: 25% if the price falls to 5p. The asymmetry is roughly 1.6:1 in favour of the upside, which is attractive for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 20‑day moving average and volume is 30% above the 20‑day average. Allocate 10% of the trading capital to this position, using a stop 15% below the entry to protect against a temporary dip.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see the redemption announcement and a clear rise in volume. If the price jumps 5% on that day, consider a small entry to capture the initial move.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks, so the setup remains bullish. If, however, a regulatory filing indicates a forced liquidation, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: The fund is a deep‑crash play at 86% below NAV, and the impending compulsory redemption gives a clear catalyst; the upside is 39% if the price recovers to NAV, while the downside is capped at a 25% loss, making it a solid contrarian bullish opportunity.
